domain_experience: 7 years implementing ERP systems for distributors
assets: deep knowledge of distributor ERP pain points
ability to translate messy operations into repeatable workflows
network_access: possible warm credibility with former ERP colleagues, partners, or operators
credibility_signals: 7 years in a narrow B2B workflow domain
unfair_advantages: better than a generic AI builder at identifying expensive distributor ERP pain
can frame solutions in operator language instead of generic AI language

binding_constraints: no outbound removes the fastest acquisition path
no content removes the main alternative acquisition path
no client work removes the fastest validation path
3 hours per week limits selling and build throughput
30-day revenue target is incompatible with product-first discovery under these constraints

opportunities.title: ERP exception audit plus weekly action pack
opportunities.thesis: Use AI to accelerate manual review of ERP exports and deliver a weekly action pack for distributor ops leaders.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Strong niche judgment about which ERP exceptions actually matter to distributor operators.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Requires client work and selling, both of which the user currently rejects.
validation_plan: Draft a one-page offer for a weekly ERP exception pack.
Test pricing at $500-$1,500 with 5 warm contacts.
Require at least 1 paid pilot before building software.
kill_criteria: Prospects want fully custom work instead of a repeatable exception pack

opportunities.title: Narrow distributor ERP copilot product
opportunities.thesis: Build a narrow AI product that flags reorder, stockout, backorder, or master-data issues from ERP exports.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Domain expertise makes the product framing more credible than a generic ops AI tool.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Product-first is too slow for first revenue in 30 days without audience, outbound, or partner distribution.
validation_plan: Do not pursue unless a manual pilot shows repeated demand across 2-3 buyers.
Confirm one repeated exception workflow before coding.
Avoid ERP integrations until buyers request them.
kill_criteria: No repeated workflow across 3 discovery conversations
No one agrees to pay for the manual version first

opportunities.title: Partner-led ERP exception pack: concierge first, tool later
opportunities.thesis: Sell or license one narrow distributor ERP exception workflow through warm partners first, then turn the repeated delivery process into software.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Combines real niche knowledge with a more realistic distribution wedge than product-first selling.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Still requires relaxing at least one constraint, especially around warm outreach or light pilot work.
validation_plan: Relax exactly one constraint: warm outreach, one pilot, or one referral ask.
Send 5 warm messages to ERP-adjacent contacts within 7 days.
Target 2 calls or 1 paid pilot at $500-$1,500.
kill_criteria: User refuses to relax any constraint
Fewer than 2 serious responses from 5 warm contacts
No paid pilot or partner interest within 30 days

rejected_paths.title: Generic AI ERP dashboard
rejected_paths.reason: Weak differentiation and no credible initial customer path under the current constraints.

rejected_paths.title: Pure content business
rejected_paths.reason: The user refuses content creation and has no audience advantage.

rejected_paths.title: Broad ERP consulting offer
rejected_paths.reason: Fastest route to money, but directly conflicts with the user's dislike of client work.

next_actions: Decide within 24 hours whether to relax one constraint.
If not, do not build a product for this 30-day goal.
If yes, test a one-page partner or pilot offer with 5 warm contacts in 7 days.
Only build an internal tool after a paid pilot or clear partner pull.
